title: Associate Director, Government Affairs & Policy, Italy
description:
At Johnson \& Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.


As guided by Our Credo, Johnson \& Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson \& Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

**Job Function:**

Communications \& Corporate/External Affairs**Job Sub Function:**

Government Affairs \& Policy**Job Category:**


Professional**All Job Posting Locations:**


Pomezia, Roma, Italy**Job Description:**


The Associate Director, Government Affairs \& Public Policy leads the development and execution of the country‑level public policy and government affairs strategy for the medical technology sector. The role is accountable for shaping a favorable policy, and healthcare environment that supports patient access to medical technologies, innovation, sustainability, and system resilience. The successful candidate will act as a company spokesperson, representing J\&J MedTech in the political and policy arena in Italy.


Acting as the senior public policy lead at country level, she or he serves as a trusted advisor to internal leadership and a credible counterpart to policymakers, authorities, and external stakeholders, while ensuring full alignment with regional and European policy priorities.

**Responsibilities:**

**Strategic Public Policy Leadership**

* Define and lead the country public policy and government affairs strategy in alignment with sector priorities and long‑term healthcare system objectives.
* Monitor and analyze legislative, political developments relevant to the environment in which the company operates, anticipating and shaping national healthcare, industrial, and procurement policies impacting medical technologies.
* Translate complex policy developments into strategic insights, risks, and opportunities for senior leadership.
* Provide thought leadership on key topics such as healthcare system reform, value‑based healthcare, sustainability, digital health, data governance, and healthcare modernization.

**Senior Government \& Stakeholder Engagement**

* Serve as the primary senior interface with national and regional policymakers, government officials, and public institutions.
* Build and maintain long‑term, trusted relationships with policy makers and other relevant external stakeholders.
* Represent the sector in high‑impact policy debates and negotiations.

**Trade Associations \& External Leadership**

* Lead country engagement with national and regional trade associations, coalitions, and multi‑stakeholder platforms.
* Influence the development of industry positions, advocacy strategies, and policy narratives.
* Align national positions with European frameworks to ensure a coherent and credible MedTech voice.
* Where relevant, hold leadership or governance roles within associations or external forums.

**Internal Strategic Advisory \& Cross‑Functional Leadership**

* Act as a strategic advisor to country and regional leadership on public policy and institutional matters.
* Drive strong cross‑functional collaboration with Regulatory Affairs, Market Access, Medical Affairs, Communications, Legal, and Commercial teams.
* Ensure alignment between public policy priorities and broader business, access, and innovation strategies.

**Qualifications:**


This is a role where the appointed candidate will be able to bring together and utilize a broad array of their skills and experiences. They will be afforded autonomy to establish and build their network of key contacts and liaise with senior stakeholders internally and externally.

* **Government Affairs Experience:** 8\+ years of government affairs or relevant experience. An in\-depth knowledge of legislative and regulatory processes and political environment across Italy. Ability to engage and develop relationships with government stakeholders. Good understating of European policy making, international policy and Italian trade relationships are desirable.
* **Relevant Industry Experience:** Experience understanding and influencing policy matters within MedTech and/or pharmaceutical/healthcare industries are required.
* **Business Orientation and Impact:** A demonstrated ability to understand business goals and work collaboratively with business leaders to support and advance those goals. A track record of effectively engaging with business executives, policy\-makers, multi\-lateral organizations, and other internal and external constituencies to deliver meaningful and results \-driven policy outcomes.
* **Drive, Agility and Collaboration:** Self\-motivated leader who enjoys a challenging, fast\-paced environment; who can drive complex projects and provide strategic direction; who has the desire and willingness to always continue evolving best practices; who is highly organized and adaptable, and can independently prioritize and deliver under tight deadlines; who can think creatively when the situation is fluid, and the solution unclear; and who brings a collaborative, team\-oriented approach to their work.
* **Executive Presence \& Communication:** Strong presentation style with excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to quickly earn the trust and engender confidence with key internal and external stakeholders.
* **Generative and agentic AI‑savvy:** Proactive and compliant use of AI‑enabled tools to strengthen policy intelligence, stakeholder engagement, and strategic foresight.
* **Alignment to J\&J’s Values \& Credo:** An individual who is engaged and inspired by J\&J’s values and Credo, and the important work we do every day for the patients and others who use our products and services.

**The following characteristics are desirable:**

* An exceptional leader, who is known for first\-class collaboration, partnership, humility and delivering on his/her leadership commitments.
* Talented at strategic thinking, but willing to execute on tactical tasks.
* Be organizationally savvy \- know how to navigate a large organization to produce results yet is also a hands\-on self\-starter who can work in a complex, multi\-stakeholder environment
* Excellent organizational skills to manage multiple projects, deal with ambiguity and manage relationships at senior levels, both externally and internally.
* Self\-motivated and passionate about building relationships and delivering quality results across the organization.
* A creative problem solver, with the flexibility to adapt to changing circumstances and priorities.

**Education:**

* A university degree is required. Master’s in public policy or law or healthcare management or MBA are desirable.

**Required Skills:**

European Policies, Healthcare Industry, Legislative Affairs, MedTech, Regulatory Processes**Preferred Skills:**


Advocacy Communications, Budget Management, Community Outreach, Compliance Management, Corporate Communications Strategy, Corporate Management, Government Relations, Leverages Information, Mentorship, Negotiation, Organizing, Public Affairs, Public Policies, Regulatory Development, Relationship Building, Representing, Stakeholder Engagement, Technical Credibility**The anticipated base pay range for this position is:**


€70,700\.00 \- €122,015\.00**Benefits:**


In addition to base pay, we offer the following benefits\*: an annual bonus with set target (% of pay) depending on pay grade / location, where the actual amount is based on the employees’ and companies’ performance of the previous calendar year, or sales commissions. Moreover, we offer vacation days, parental leave for a minimum of 12 weeks, bereavement leave, caregiver leave, volunteer leave, well\-being reimbursement, programs for financial, physical and mental health. We also offer service anniversary and recognition awards, and subject to the terms of their respective plans, employees \- and in some location’s eligible dependents \- can participate in several insurance plans. For more information, visit Employee benefits \| Supporting well\-being \& career growth \| Johnson \& Johnson Careers.

* This is for informative purposes only. Amounts and actual benefits may vary by location and are subject to change.

title: SUPPLIER QUALITY ASSURANCE MANAGER
description:
**SUPPLIER QUALITY ASSURANCE MANAGER**



**What You’ll Be Doing**



We are looking for a **Supplier Quality Assurance Manager** who will play a key role in strengthening and evolving our end‑to‑end quality approach across suppliers of packaging materials, raw materials, warehousing and franchise partners.



Reporting to the E2E Quality Manager, you will be responsible for driving a shift from a reactive to a proactive quality mindset, ensuring supplier compliance, continuous improvement, and strong collaboration with both internal and external stakeholders. You will also contribute to structuring and developing the team while interacting frequently with international counterparts.

  


**✅** **Your Activities Will Include**


* Plan and execute supplier audits (packaging materials, raw materials, warehouses, franchises), ensuring compliance with quality and safety standards
* Manage supplier non‑conformities, corrective action plans, and follow‑up activities
* Evaluate and approve packaging materials and raw materials, ensuring quality, safety, and regulatory compliance
* Support and validate product and packaging claims, in collaboration with marketing, procurement and regulatory stakeholders
* Drive supplier performance improvement and efficiency initiatives, promoting continuous improvement
* Work closely with internal functions (Quality Plant, Pack \& Brew Plant, Procurement, Supply Chain, Material Management, Marketing for NPD)
* Contribute to team structuring and development, potentially managing 1–2 collaborators
* Support the evolution of quality processes by providing structure, visibility and a medium‑long‑term strategic perspective

  



**Who We’re Looking For**


* You hold a University Degree in Food Science \& Technology, Quality \& Food Safety, Chemistry, Biology or a related scientific field
* You have at least 7 years of experience in Quality roles within manufacturing environments (F\&B and/or packaging industries preferred)
* You have proven experience in supplier audits, quality systems and supplier management
* You have solid knowledge of packaging materials and raw materials
* You have experienze in claim management and cross\-functional decision\-making
* You have the ability to read processes end\-to\-end and transform them from reactive to structured and proactive.
* You have a fluent English proficiency (written and spoken)
* You have an Auditor certification (ISO 19011\)

  


**In Return We Offer**


* Flexible working hours, allowing you to organize your schedule independently, managing your time according to your goals and priorities.
* Opportunities for growth within an international environment.
* Structured onboarding plan to support your integration, along with dedicated training programs for our sales force (*Sales Academy \#RaiseTheCap)*;
* Our Welfare and Wellbeing services.

  



**Where?**



Your base will be our HeadQuarter in Rome


**Environment That You Will find**



We are committed to nurturing a workplace where we celebrate and respect difference, and support everyone to be true to who they are! At Asahi Europe and International we believe you can SHINE AS YOU ARE \- no matter your age, gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disabilities, religion, or beliefs. We want to support you to be courageous in your individuality and to know that whoever you are and whatever your circumstances, you can belong with us without having to conform.

  


**What next?**


* Our Talent Acquisition Team will assess your application and if you have what we’re looking for, you'll be contacted for an initial phone screen;
* If we like what we hear, you’ll be invited to an interview with a member of the Talent Acquisition team and your future Manager!

title: Junior Process Engineer - Rome
description:
WSP has a new opportunity for a **Junior Process Engineer** to join our Engineering Services team on a full\-time contract basis, based in one of our offices in Rome.

**WSP employs 83\.000 professionals** worldwide in the engineering sector.


In Italy, WSP has 400 employees and has been operating in the sector for over 40 years.


We are a global leader in Environmental Consulting \- made stronger with the recent acquisition of Wood’s Environment \& Infrastructure business that together with Golder have world\-class expertise in environmental, social and governance (ESG), climate resiliency \& sustainability (CRS), and earth sciences. We have over 20,000 multidisciplinary professionals worldwide collaborating daily to protect and conserve natural landscapes, create firm foundations for communities to stand on, and champion sustainable development to combat climate change.

**YOUR NEW ROLE, WHATS INVOLVED?**

* Under close supervision, you will perform the routine aspects of engineering assignments requiring knowledge and application of basic engineering principles.
* Using prescribed methods, performs specific and limited portions of engineering assignments.
* Compiles, verifies and correlates data relating to process engineering problems.
* Assists in conceptual designs, studies, reports, proposals and preparation of process deliverables.
* Performs calculations, simulations and basic preliminary analyses.
* Uses computer programs as directed.
* Assists in detailed development of established design concepts for process systems and equipment.
* Participates in preparation of material requisitions, specifications and other related documents.
* Coordinates assigned work with other technical personnel as directed.
* Assists in development and verification of project controls documents and status rep.

**WHAT IF IT WAS YOU?**

* Basic knowledge of water chemistry and water treatment processes.
* Basic technical knowledge of systems calculations, equipment, applications of methods, process flow diagrams (PFD’s), piping and instrumentation diagrams (P\&ID’s), plant layout, materials, process engineering calculations.
* Basic knowledge of industry/ regulatory codes and standards, and design and/or layout criteria pertinent to the process discipline.
* Learning to be consultant and/or designer \- Able to produce quality deliverables within scope, schedule and budget. Course corrections along the way are expected, especially for new grads, however ability to work within scope, schedule and budget, and/or communicate issues on tasks owned is a core expectation of this level.
* Decisions: Few technical decisions called for and these will be of routine nature with ample precedent or clearly defined procedures and guidelines. Work requires accuracy in calculations, completeness of data and adherence to prescribed analysis, design or computational methods.
* Receives operational supervision and technical guidance from more senior engineers.
* May provide technical direction as appropriate to others who perform specific design support assignments.
* May assign and check work of less experienced personnel.
* Work in immediate work group and interfaces with other disciplines as specifically directed.
* Able to follow through on commitments
* Ability to articulate well in written communications evolving into technical writing proficiency
* Practices safe behaviors in the field and office settings
* Willing to perform any task put in front of them
* Accepts and seeks out coaching and feedback – Shows willingness and ability to apply feedback
* Able to manage personal workload and multiple commitments
* Demonstrates growth potential both from soft skill and technical skill perspectives
* Understands the importance of building a network across the business unit and although may need assistance keeping 100% busy, by the end of their time at this level has the beginning of an established network of technology peers, project and task managers feeding them workload.

**Education and experience**

* Degree in engineering discipline.
* 2 years’ experience.

**WHAT YOU’LL LIKE ABOUT US:**

* A working environment inspired by **diversity**,driven by inclusion.
* Our flexible working policy \- We recognise the importance of **work\-life balance** and encourage you to find yours.
* WSP is about making the world around us a **better place**, including the environment, transport infrastructure, buildings and land use.
* Excellent **career** prospects. We're growing fast, and every day we strive to outdo ourselves. We are open to your ideas and want to try **new** things. We have a supportive environment that invests in your **development** and training.
* WSP is about embracing a culture of **collaboration** with colleagues who stand out for their **skills**. Come and discover our fantastic team!

**OUR RECRUITMENT PROCESS:**

* Telephone interview: We'll start with a telephone interview to get to know you better and discuss your experience.
* Manager Interview: You will meet with our TA team and the department manager to assess your technical and cultural skills.
* Interview with the Business Unit Director: A meeting with the Business Unit Director to discuss your potential role and our strategic vision.

**ABOUT US:**


We are one of the world's leading engineering and professional services firms. Our trusted employees share a common goal of making a positive and lasting impact on the communities we serve through a culture of innovation, integrity and inclusion.

**Are you hesitating to apply? Go for it!** We are proud to promote diversity, equal opportunities and professional integration within our teams, and we are convinced that our differences and an inclusive working environment contribute to our development. All applications will be carefully considered.

**\#WeAreWSP**

**\#WeAreVisioneers**


\#LI\-DA1
company_description: What if you could do the kind of work the world needs? With us, you can.

title: AI & Data Governance and Trust Manager
description:
**Company Description**  

MSX has been a trusted partner to leading vehicle manufacturers, their retailers, and mobility organizations globally for more than 30 years. Our unwavering commitment is to help our clients transform their businesses and effectively manage operations in the areas of: Sales Performance; Repair Optimization and Compliance; Parts and Accessories Sales Performance; and Consumer Engagement. With our global teams, industry expertise, and the power of technology, we design and deliver tailored, sustainable, and innovative solutions and services that help our clients optimize their operations and captivate their customers.

 **Job Description**  

As AI \& Data Governance and Trust Manager you will be the operational backbone of responsible AI and Data management within the AI \& Data Center of Excellence. In this role you ensure that AI systems and data assets are deployed, managed, and governed in compliance with regulatory requirements, ethical standards, and enterprise risk frameworks.


This position focuses on building and operationalizing governance structures that enable trust, transparency, and accountability across the **Data Lifecycle** \- from collection and storage to processing, sharing, retention, and disposal. As the specialist you will work closely with the AI Solutions Architect to embed compliance guardrails into technical patterns and with the AI \& Data Transformation Lead to integrate governance into the organization's data culture.

**Your responsibilities will include**


AI Governance \& Risk Management

* Design and maintain the AI Risk Registry, classifying AI systems by risk tier (EU AI Act alignment).
* Define approval workflows, documentation requirements, and lifecycle checkpoints for AI initiatives.
* Monitor AI model performance for drift, fairness, and compliance post\-deployment.
* Serve as the primary point of contact for AI\-related regulatory inquiries and audits.
* Conduct AI ethics and bias assessments in collaboration with technical teams.


Data Governance \& Lifecycle Management

* Operationalize the Data Governance Council, including agenda setting, decision tracking, and stakeholder coordination.
* Define and maintain Critical Data Element (CDE) definitions, ownership, and quality baselines.
* Support Master Data Management (MDM) pilot initiatives with governance frameworks and data stewardship models.
* Define governance requirements for metadata, cataloging, and data quality tooling; partner with Data Engineering/IT on platform ownership and operations.
* Establish data retention, archival, and disposal policies aligned with GDPR and enterprise compliance standards.
* Collaborate with IT and Security teams to ensure data access controls, encryption, and audit trails are in place.


Policy Development \& Compliance

* Draft, review, and maintain AI and Data policies, standards, and procedures, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements (EU AI Act, GDPR, industry\-specific mandates).
* Conduct compliance gap assessments and recommend remediation plans.
* Provide governance training and awareness programs for business and technical stakeholders.
* Maintain a governance knowledge base and decision log for transparency and auditability.


Cross\-Functional Collaboration

* Partner with the AI Solutions Architect to validate that technical designs meet governance and compliance requirements.
* Work with the AI \& Data Transformation Lead to embed AI \& Data governance frameworks into capability\-building programs and community initiatives.
* Collaborate with Legal and Information Security teams to ensure alignment on risk management and regulatory obligations.
* Support Value Streams and Support Functions with advisory for AI and Data governance.
* Engage with external auditors, regulators, and industry bodies as AI and Data governance subject matter expert.

 **Success in this role means:**

* Governance frameworks adopted across AI and data initiatives
* Audit\-ready compliance evidence and risk posture
* Reduced delivery friction through clear decision rights and guardrails
* Measurable improvement in data quality for Critical Data Elements

  

**Qualifications**  

* ***Education:***Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Systems, or Business Administration (Advanced degrees in Ethics, Risk Management, or Regulatory Compliance are highly valued)
* ***Experience:***Essential experience includes **5\+ years in Data Management, Data Governance, or Information Risk roles**, with a proven track record of operationalizing enterprise\-wide governance frameworks
* ***Skills:***The role requires a rare blend of technical literacy and regulatory fluency. Necessary skills include
	+ the ability to translate complex regulations into actionable business policies,
	+ expertise in Master Data Management (MDM) and Critical Data Element (CDE) definition
	+ strong proficiency in risk classification methodologies.
* ***Interpersonally:*** You possess superior collaborative skills to partner with Solutions Architects and Legal teams, and the executive presence to act as a subject matter expert during audits and regulatory inquiries.
* ***Languages:*** Professional **proficiency in English required**; Italian and/or additional European languages are a plus.

  

**Additional Information**  

With over 5,000 employees based in more than 80 countries across the globe, our teams provide industry leading expertise that spans:

* Consumer Engagement
* Parts, Accessories \& Service Performance
* Actionable Insights
* Repair Optimization \& Compliance
* Learning Solutions
* Distribution \& Sales Performance


Our proven track record means that we now partner with almost every car manufacturer on the market.


The MSX Purpose


To empower Movers and Makers to thrive in our ever\-changing world


The MSX Mission


To harness our expertise in mobility, the creativity of our global teams, and the power of technology, to craft tailored, sustainable and innovative solutions.


The MSX Vision


To be the clients’ first choice, recognized for our operational excellence and commitment to driving change and innovation in the mobility industry.


MSX is an equal opportunities employer and encourages applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, neurodiversity or other personal characteristics and backgrounds, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, or marital and parental status. As users of the Disability Confident scheme, we interview all disabled applicants who meet the minimum criteria for the vacancy.

title: Label Change Coordinator - Fix Term Contract
description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose\-driven, world\-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.  

  

Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.  

  

Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance\-focused culture.

**About the role**


We are seeking to hire a new team member for the Label Change Team, reporting to the Label Change Manager, to support the growing activities of our team at the Aprilia site. This role is part of our team expansion to meet increasing demands and enhance our operational efficiency. The successful candidate will contribute to ensuring timely and accurate label changes, supporting the overall success of our operations in Aprilia.

**Responsibilities**

* Project coordination and management: interfaces and coordinates packaging change and product launch projects, managing notification flows, priorities, and approvals.
* Data management and relationships: interfaces with E2E SO team for master data management and with other site functions involved in packaging projects.
* Artwork process management: coordinates the artwork process for the Aprilia site, ensuring compliance and quality of artworks.
* Communication and liaison: acts as a liaison between affiliates, production, and internal teams, managing activities and escalating critical issues.
* Control and verification: checks the technical correctness of artworks and participates in product launch teams to ensure customer requirements and deadlines are met.

**Your profile**

* Preferrable level of education: Pharmaceutical chemistry Degree or Graphic Design and Art Direction Degree.
* Minimum experience of 2 years in pharmaceutical regulated environment, with a basic understanding of the artwork and packaging flow.
* Knowledge of GMP standards.
* Advanced level of English: effective communication through speaking and writing.
* Good communication to ensure effective interactions with regional and global groups, ownership of assigned tasks in projects, problem solving skills.
* Software: Microsoft Office, SAP, Acrobat DC.

  

**Job Posting End Date**

  


2026\-06\-03**Equal Opportunities**
-----------------------


Haleon are committed to mobilising our purpose in a way that represents the diverse consumers and communities who rely on our brands every day. It guides us in creating an inclusive culture, where different backgrounds and views are valued and respected – all in support of understanding and best serving the needs of our consumers and unleashing the full potential of our people. It’s important to us that Haleon is a place where all our employees feel they truly belong.
  

  

During the application process, we may ask you to share some personal information, which is entirely voluntary. This information ensures we meet certain regulatory and reporting obligations and supports the development, refinement, and execution of our inclusion and belonging programmes that are open to all Haleon employees.
  

  

The personal information you provide will be kept confidential, used only for legitimate business purposes, and will never be used in making any employment decisions, including hiring decisions.

**Adjustment or Accommodations Request**
----------------------------------------


If you require a reasonable adjustment or accommodation or other assistance to apply for a job at Haleon at any stage of the application process, please let your recruiter know by providing them with a description of specific adjustments you are requesting. We’ll provide all reasonable adjustments to support you throughout the recruitment process and treat all information you provide us in confidence.

**Note to candidates**
----------------------


The Haleon recruitment team will contact you using a Haleon email account (@haleon.com). If you are not sure whether the email you received is from Haleon, please get in touch.
company_description: A health company that puts people first, we exist to deliver better everyday health with humanity.

title: QA Digital Compliance Manager
description:
**Company Description** **About AbbVie**


AbbVie's mission is to discover and deliver innovative medicines and solutions that solve serious health issues today and address the medical challenges of tomorrow. We strive to have a remarkable impact on people's lives across several key therapeutic areas including immunology, oncology and neuroscience \- and products and services in our Allergan Aesthetics portfolio. For more information about AbbVie, please visit us at www.abbvie.com. Follow @abbvie on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X and YouTube.

 **Job Description**  

The QA Digital Compliance Manager is responsible for ensuring that all digital systems, records, instruments, processes and data handling within operations adhere to relevant regulatory, legal, best practices and industry standards.


Key Responsibilities:

* Monitoring and ensuring adherence to relevant regulatory bodies (e.g. FDA, EMA) for digital content and initiatives in compliance with regulations like FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11\. Keeping updating with evolving digital compliance regulations and trends (eg Annex 22 about AI).
* Ensuring and maintaining Quality oversight of data integrity and traceability across computerized systems lifecycle such as manufacturing execution system, and laboratory information management systems (LIMS), electronic lab notebooks (ELN/LES), and instrument software.
* Support the implementation of proper cybersecurity measures to protect data and prevent unauthorized access or tampering.
* Supporting as Subject Matter Expert the training to employees and stakeholders on digital compliance requirements and best practices and the audit of digital systems and assure the adherence to GMP, company policies and other relevant standards, including risk assessments, Supplier qualification and periodic System Audit Trail reviews.
* Ensuring software and digital tools and systems used in operations are validated and documented according to regulatory and company requirements through the review and approval of validation documents
* Supporting the development, review, and update of digital compliance policies and procedures.
* Collaborating with Global and Local IT, BTS and all relevant functions to maintain compliant digital strategies.
* Investigating and responding to non\-compliance events. Contributing to change management and lifecycle handling.

  

**Qualifications**  

* Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences, IT or related field; master’s degree preferred.
* 5/10\+ years of experience in digital compliance applied in pharmaceutical industry
* Strong knowledge of pharmaceutical regulations (FDA, EMA, etc.)
* Excellent communication and training skills.
* Fluent English language
* Analytical mindset and attention to detail.
* Experience in managerial roles in complex organizations.

  

**Additional Information**  

AbbVie is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to operating with integrity, driving innovation, transforming lives and serving our community. Equal Opportunity Employer/Veterans/Disabled.


US \& Puerto Rico only \- to learn more, visit https://www.abbvie.com/join\-us/equal\-employment\-opportunity\-employer.html


US \& Puerto Rico applicants seeking a reasonable accommodation, click here to learn more:


https://www.abbvie.com/join\-us/reasonable\-accommodations.html

title: Production Quality Specialist - fixed term (RED PROGRAM)
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.


The Resources Empowerment and Development (R.E.D.) Program is designed for people who seek a performance\-based culture that is built on meritocracy \- ambitious, hard\-working, passionate employees who deliver results will be rewarded.


This program will start in Ferentino and foresees secondments in various functional groups (Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain etc.) where selected candidates will experience accelerated career opportunities throughout job rotations, having the chance to be part of different teams.


The ultimate goal is that of creating formed professionals to work in key teams: Production Quality Specialist.

  

DESCRIPTION:  

Join our high\-performing team at Thermo Fisher Scientific, where you'll ensure quality compliance across manufacturing operations while advancing our mission to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. As a Quality Specialist I, you'll provide essential quality oversight through detailed documentation review, process monitoring, and collaboration with cross\-functional teams. You'll help maintain GMP compliance, investigate quality events, and drive continuous improvement initiatives while working in a collaborative environment focused on delivering life\-changing products to our customers.  

  

REQUIREMENTS:

* Bachelor's Degree required, preferred Fields of Study: Chemistry, Biology, Engineering, Life Sciences, or related scientific field
* Additional relevant certifications (e.g. ISO auditor, Six Sigma) are advantageous
* Fluency in English required; additional language skills beneficial
* Proficiency with quality systems software and Microsoft Office applications
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities with keen attention to detail
* Outstanding verbal and written communication skills
* Commitment to maintaining a quality\-focused culture and driving continuous improvement
* Collaborate with the Quality System Department to review SOPs
* Conduct and manage departmental training
* Prepare and present compliance documents and procedures
* Manage internal, customer, and regulatory audits
* Support continuous improvement initiatives
* Provide documentary support to the production department
* Proven ability to review and approve GMP documentation, batch records, and quality records
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: IM Transcend - Sr Analyst, Signavio
description:
At Johnson \& Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.


As guided by Our Credo, Johnson \& Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson \& Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

**Job Function:**

Technology Product \& Platform Management**Job Sub Function:**

Technical Product Management**Job Category:**


Scientific/Technology**All Job Posting Locations:**


Beerse, Antwerp, Belgium, Latina, Italy, Little Island, Cork, Ireland**Job Description:**

**About Innovative Medicine**


Our expertise in Innovative Medicine is informed and inspired by patients, whose insights fuel our science\-based advancements. Visionaries like you work on teams that save lives by developing the medicines of tomorrow. Join us in developing treatments, finding cures, and pioneering the path from lab to life while championing patients every step of the way. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/innovative\-medicine.


Transcend is a global business transformation program that will modernize our foundational transactional processes and harmonize them into one “clean” ERP as a standardized platform for growth and efficiency gains.


This program will simplify the Pharmaceutical ERP landscape from 7 to 1, standardizing processes to have a cost\-effective, fit\-for\-purpose digital backbone that will enable us to support the Pharmaceutical business with agility. Additionally, the program will remediate the end\-of\-life system exposure that we risk towards the end of this decade.

***Please note that this role is available across multiple countries and may be posted under different requisition numbers to comply with local requirements. While you are welcome to apply to any or all of the postings, we recommend focusing on the specific country(s) that align with your preferred location(s):***

*United States \- Requisition Number: R\-072629*

*Italy, Belgium, and Ireland\- Requisition Number: R\-072109*

*Canada\- Requisition Number: R\-072621*

***Remember, whether you apply to one or all of these requisition numbers, your applications will be considered as a single submission.***


We are searching for the best talent for a Transcend: Signavio Program Senior Analyst! This hybrid position will be located in Latina, Italy. Alternate hybrid locations may be considered in Titusville, New Jersey; Raritan, New Jersey; Toronto, Canada; Latina, Italy; Cork, Ireland; Beerse, Belgium. Alternate locations and Remote work may be considered for the right candidate.


Are you ready to be part of a multi\-year global business transformation initiative that will transform and standardize our core business processes and products across Plan, Source, Make, Quality, Deliver, and Data, and enable our integrated data strategy through a single SAP S/4 HANA instance? Apply today for this exciting opportunity to shape and set the future direction for supporting and improving our new process across all areas.


We are seeking a detail\-oriented Signavio Program Analyst to maintain and support our Signavio process management platform. The role focuses on day\-to\-day system administration, process repository quality, user support and enablement, and close collaboration with process owners and IT to ensure the platform delivers reliable, accurate process documentation and analytics.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Administer and maintain the Signavio environment: user accounts, permissions, libraries, templates, and version control.
* Maintain process repository hygiene: enforce naming conventions, metadata completeness, process ownership, and archival policies.
* Provide 1st/2nd\-line user support for Signavio issues (triage, escalate, track tickets, perform root\-cause checks).
* Apply and manage configuration changes, updates, and platform releases in coordination with IT and the Signavio vendor.
* Support integrations and data flows (e.g., process mining tools, identity/SSO, reporting platforms) by coordinating with technical teams and validating data consistency.
* Validate BPMN models and ensure process documentation meets agreed governance and quality standards.
* Produce and maintain user guides, how‑to articles, admin runbooks, and training materials; deliver periodic training and onboarding sessions.
* Monitor usage, run regular audits, and produce reports on repository health and adoption metrics.
* Capture user feedback and support prioritization of enhancements, workflow improvements, and cleanup activities.
* Support governance processes: assist with change requests, maintain access logs, and help enforce compliance and audit requirements.

**Qualifications**

**EDUCATION:**

* Minimum of High School or equivalent diploma/degree is required; Bachelor’s / University or equivalent degree strongly preferred

**Experience and skills:**

**Required:**

* 4 years of relevant work experience with at least 2 years experience supporting or administering a process management/Business Process Mapping platform (Signavio experience preferred).
* Strong working knowledge of process modeling and BPM fundamentals.
* Familiarity with basic integration concepts (APIs, SSO/identity) and data validation practices.
* Excellent written and verbal communication and documentation skills.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Hands\-on experience specifically with Signavio Suite (Process Manager, Workflow Accelerator, Process Intelligence).
* Certifications in BPMN, Signavio, ITIL, or related disciplines.
* Experience with process\-mining, RPA, or analytics tools (e.g., Process Intelligence, Celonis, Power BI).
* Experience working in regulated or highly governed environments.

**Other:**

* Requires proficiency in English (written and verbal) to communicate effectively and professionally; proficiency in German, Dutch, or Italian is helpful
* May require up to 15% domestic and international travel depending on work location and business needs
* Requires the ability, through our flexible work policy, to work on\-site a minimum of three days each week, with the option for two remote workdays each week, remote work may be available for the right candidate


\#LI\-Hybrid


Johnson \& Johnson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by federal, state or local law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.


Johnson \& Johnson is committed to providing an interview process that is inclusive of our applicants’ needs. If you are an individual with a disability and would like to request an accommodation, external applicants please contact us via https://www.jnj.com/contact\-us/careers, internal employees contact AskGS to be directed to your accommodation resource.

**Required Skills:**

Business Process Mapping, Business Process Modeling, Data Validation**Preferred Skills:**


Agile Product Development, Analytical Reasoning, Coaching, Collaborating, Competitive Landscape Analysis, Critical Thinking, Customer Alignment, Demand Forecasting, Human\-Computer Interaction (HCI), Organizing, Product Development, Product Improvements, Product Strategies, Requirements Analysis, Research and Development, Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), Software Development Management, Stakeholder Management, Technical Credibility, Technical Writing, Technologically Savvy**The anticipated pay range for this position, in the primary posting location, is:**


€48,600\.00 \- €77,395\.00**The anticipated pay ranges for additional locations are:**


Belgium – The anticipated base pay range for this position is EUR72,500– EUR115,230  

Ireland– The anticipated base pay range for this position is EUR63,300 – EUR101,315**Benefits:**


In addition to base pay, we offer the following benefits\*: an annual bonus with set target (% of pay) depending on pay grade / location, where the actual amount is based on the employees’ and companies’ performance of the previous calendar year, or sales commissions. Moreover, we offer vacation days, parental leave for a minimum of 12 weeks, bereavement leave, caregiver leave, volunteer leave, well\-being reimbursement, programs for financial, physical and mental health. We also offer service anniversary and recognition awards, and subject to the terms of their respective plans, employees \- and in some location’s eligible dependents \- can participate in several insurance plans. For more information, visit Employee benefits \| Supporting well\-being \& career growth \| Johnson \& Johnson Careers.

* This is for informative purposes only. Amounts and actual benefits may vary by location and are subject to change.

title: Technology Senior Architect - Italy
description:
**We help the world run better**  

At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. **What you’ll do**

  

We are seeking for a curious, skilled and motivated Technology Architect to join our dynamic Customer Engagement and Adoption organization at SAP with the opportunity of a rapid growth in the technical area. As a Technology Architect, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the digital transformation journey of our customers by supporting the positioning and selling cycle of new SAP technologies. You will also lead the design and implementation of innovative solutions based on SAP technologies.

  

In particular you will:

  

* Collaborate with customers, business stakeholders, and technology teams to understand business objectives, identify opportunities, and define technology strategies aligned with SAP's product portfolio
* Design end\-to\-end enterprise solutions that meet business requirements, considering scalability, performance, security, and integration aspects
* Develop architectural governance, roadmaps, and implementation plans to guide the execution of technology initiatives
* Provide leadership and guidance to build architecture teams throughout the solution delivery lifecycle
* Evaluate emerging technologies and industry trends to identify opportunities for innovation and continuous improvement
* Ensure compliance with architectural standards and best practices, promoting reusability and adherence to SAP's architectural principles.
* Conduct technology assessments and facilitate decision\-making processes to support solution adoption and investment prioritization
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to address complex business challenges, resolve technical issues, and ensure successful project delivery
* Contribute to selling cycles of SAP Technology solutions (e.g., RISE, BTP, AI and Toolchain) while maximizing customer value and adoption
* Play the Technical Architect role in SAP Implementation Project being capable to deliver activities like definition of scope of work, customer project presentation, design technical aspects of solutions, write Business Blueprint, lead offshore resources, design project GANTT, produce high quality deliverables, identify new business opportunities to enlarge SAP footprint, etc.
* Play the role of “Expert on Demand” while supporting SAP Partners in their implementations providing best practices and guidelines to ensure a successful SAP solution implementation
* Stay updated with the latest advancements in SAP technology offerings, actively participating in professional development activities (e.g. AI topics)

 **What you bring**

  

* Degree in STEM (or equivalent practical experience)
* \+5 years of customer\-facing SAP consulting experience
* \+5 years in solution design, enterprise architecture, and/or SAP implementation (e.g. S/4HANA conversions)
* Strong knowledge of SAP Technology products (releases, platform interdependencies, etc.)
* Good understanding of SAP solutions operations (application \+ technical) in cloud environments
* Solid understanding of platform stacks (storage, virtualization, hardware, networking)
* Good knowledge of at least one hyperscaler (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
* SAP BTP knowledge and experience

 **Soft Skills \& Personal Attributes**

  

* Native or excellent command of Italian
* Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non‑technical audiences
* Fluent English, both written and spoken
* Highly self‑motivated and well organized, with strong ownership and accountability
* Strong innovation mindset, with openness to new ideas and approaches
* Excellent problem‑solving skills, particularly in complex, multi‑vendor environments
* Cultural awareness and proven experience working in multinational contexts
* Availability and flexibility to travel on short notice

 **Nice\-to\-Have Skills**

  

* SAP certifications (C\_TADM70\_22, C\_TADM\_23 or similar, C\_ADBTP or similar)
* Experience with IaaS providers, SAP Premium Suppliers, or data centers
* Experience in SAP HANA / S/4HANA performance analysis
* Cybersecurity and compliance expertise
* Experience with SAP AI implementations (e.g. SAP Joule or similar)
* SAP Application Lifecycle Management / SAP Operations experience

 **Bring out your best**  

SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end\-to\-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose\-driven and future\-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.
  

  

**We win with inclusion**  

SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well\-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.  

  

SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e\-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.  

  

For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.  

  

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements.  

  

Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor. **AI Usage in the Recruitment Process**
For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process.
Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.  

  

Requisition ID: 446584 \| Work Area: Consulting and Professional Services \| Expected Travel: 0 \- 10% \| Career Status: Professional \| Employment Type: Regular Full Time \| Additional Locations: \#LI\-Hybrid
company_description: SAP's purpose is to help the world run better and improve people's lives with sustainability at the core.

title: Equipment Validation Lead
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover impactful work, innovative thinking and a culture dedicated to working the right way, for the right reasons \- with the customer always top of mind. The work we do matters, like helping customers find cures for cancer, protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe and delivering COVID\-19 solutions. As the world leader in serving science, with the largest investment in R\&D in the industry, our colleagues are empowered to realize their full potential as part of a fast\-growing, global organization that values passion and unique contributions. Our commitment to our colleagues across the globe is to provide the resources and opportunities they need to make a difference in our world while building a fulfilling career with us.

**Location/Division Specific Information**
------------------------------------------


Ferentino, Italy

**Discover Impactful Work:**
----------------------------


Ensure the execution of the qualification activities of the Site Equipment reported in the written protocol. Assure the tests are in line with the main guidelines and GMP.

**A day in the Life:**
----------------------

* Perform the analysis and evaluation of technical documentation for system to be qualified in order to acquire information necessary for the issue of protocols and the execution of activities.
* Issue of qualification protocols (IQ\-OQ\-PQ Protocols), through research and acquisition of data and information necessary to define their contents.
* Lead the external consultants in order to assure the correct qualification of the system/equipment (IQ\-OQ\-PQ Protocols)
* Carry out the analysis of the qualification activities and issue the qualification reports (IQ\-OQ\-PQ report), in compliance with current regulations (current EU GMP, US GMP) and company quality standards.
* Support the Quality Department in preparing Customer Audits and Regulatory Inspections, providing necessary information and documentation.
* Collaborate with other Site Departments in order to achieve the goal of the company’s projects.
* Participate in calls/meetings with customers/clients to support the discussion on topics related to equipment qualification.
* Collaborate with the Supervisor to close on time all assigned project and review all the documents prepared by the qualification team.

**Keys to Success:**
--------------------

**Education**
-------------

* Technical / Scientific Degree (Mechanical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, CTF, Biomedical Engineering, Pharmacy, Industrial Chemistry)

**Experience**
--------------

* Experience in pharmaceutical industries
* At least 5 years’ experience in Equipment Qualification Department

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities**
--------------------------------

* Knowledge of the main pharmaceutical machines/systems
* Fluent in English
* Good Team\-worker
* Good problem\-solving abilities
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite and electronic systems such as Documentum and Trackwise
* Be prepared to work with conflicting priorities, under time constraints and in a dynamic team environment

**Benefits**
------------


We offer competitive remuneration, annual incentive plan bonus, healthcare, and a range of employee benefits. Thermo Fisher Scientific offers employment with an innovative, forward\-thinking organization, and outstanding career and development prospects. We offer an exciting company culture that stands for integrity, intensity, involvement, and innovation!


Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us. As one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- Integrity, Intensity, Innovation and Involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#StartYourStory at Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.

**Apply today! http://jobs.thermofisher.com**
---------------------------------------------


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an EEO/Affirmative Action Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

**Accessibility/Disability Access**
-----------------------------------


Job Seekers with a Disability: Thermo Fisher Scientific offers accessibility service for job seekers requiring accommodations in the job application process. For example, this may include individuals requiring assistance because of hearing, vision, mobility, or cognitive impairments. If you are a job seeker with a disability, or assisting a person with a disability, and require accessibility assistance or an accommodation to apply for one of our jobs, please submit a request by telephone at 1\-855\-471\-2255\*. Please include your contact information and specific details about your required accommodation to support you during the job application process.

* This telephone line is reserved solely for job seekers with disabilities requiring accessibility assistance or an accommodation in the job application process. Messages left for other purposes, such as not being able to get into the career website, following up on an application, or other non\-disability related technical issues will not receive a response.

title: Inside Sales
Sales Account Divisione Legno zona Roma Sud
description:
Siamo leader nella distribuzione di prodotti per il fissaggio e montaggio in diversi settori. Insieme ci impegniamo a costruire una Customer Experience all'avanguardia. In questo scenario di crescita, i pilastri su cui basiamo le strategie di sviluppo del nostro business sono omnicanalità, innovazione, digitalizzazione, sostenibilità e valorizzazione del capitale umano. Condividiamo un unico obiettivo: sviluppare il cliente, contribuendo a costruire relazioni di fiducia, comprendere i bisogni dei clienti e proporre soluzioni concrete, innovative e sostenibili.

  

Dare to Build Different è la nostra purpose: per noi significa innovare, valorizzare le diversità e trasformare le competenze in impatto reale. Qui puoi osare, fare la differenza e costruire il futuro insieme a noi.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

n Würth Italia sappiamo che ci vuole coraggio per cambiare le regole del gioco.

  

Stiamo cercando un/una Sales Account Divisione Legno da inserire nella zona di Roma Sud / Pomezia.

  

Di cosa ti occuperai?

  

A supporto diretto del Regional Area Manager di riferimento, l’obiettivo del tuo ruolo è legato all’ampliamento del business, avendo la responsabilità della crescita del fatturato e dell’ampliamento della clientela, attraverso l’implementazione delle strategie aziendali e garantendo una eccellente customer experience.

  

Come sarà una tua giornata tipo?

  

Come Sales Account, non esiste una giornata tipo, perchè il dinamismo ne è l’ingrediente principale! Ti occuperai di ricercare soluzioni per ampliare il tuo portafoglio clienti di piccole strutture e/o artigiani che operano nella tua zona di competenza, e di fidelizzarli massimizzando le loro opportunità di acquisto.

  

Ai fini di raggiungere i tuoi obiettivi di business, avrai anche la possibilità di collaborare attivamente con tutti i punti di contatto, che includono sia i colleghi della parte Retail che le figure specialistiche territoriali e di settore.

  

Attraverso il tuo lavoro, i clienti potranno vivere una customer experience positiva e sempre più orientata all’utilizzo di prodotti, servizi e soluzioni innovative

**Requisiti:**
--------------

Sei la persona giusta per noi se hai:

  

Percorso formativo orientato ai dati (es. Laurea in ambito economico)


Spiccato digital mindset


Capacità analitiche e orientamento al business


Ottima capacità di networking


Buone competenze di negoziazione


Buona conoscenza della Lingua Inglese (livello minimo B1\)

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

PER SUPPORTARTI NEL LAVORO

  

Un pacchetto welfare che comprende numerose convenzioni aziendali e un supporto della persona a 360 gradi


Employee Assistance Program: supporto psicologico gratuito e confidenziale disponibile sia online sia in presenza


Servizio di consulenza informativa su tematiche legali e fiscali, erogato da professionisti/e qualificati/e


Care Manager per supportarti nella conciliazione vita\-lavoro risolvendo tematiche di natura socio\-assistenziale


Strumenti digitali che, grazie all’integrazione dell’intelligenza artificiale, renderanno lo sviluppo del cliente ancora più efficiente


Auto aziendale


Buoni pasto


INOLTRE, IN WÜRTH ITALIA POTRAI:

  

Crescere e formarti continuamente, costruendo il percorso su misura per tedalla fase di onboarding e una formazione continua nel ruolo e trasversale


Dare valore alle tue idee, partecipando a communities e a progettiche premiano la proattività e l’innovazione


Prenderti cura del tuo benessere a 360°, con Würth loves You: una piattaforma che raccoglie un piano di welfare strutturato e che soddisfa le esigenze per ogni fase di vita delle nostre persone e pensa al tuo tempo libero con convenzioni e agevolazioni.


Fare la differenza, dando vita alla nostra purpose Dare to Build Different


Vuoi conoscere meglio il mondo Würth? Scoprilo qui.

  



  

La presente selezione si intende valida anche per il collocamento mirato ex L. 68/1999\.

  

La ricerca è rivolta a uomini e donne ex L.903/77 e in osservanza del DLgs.196/03 art 7/13/2
company_description: Die Würth-Gruppe ist Weltmarktführer in der Entwicklung, der Herstellung und dem Vertrieb von Montage- und Befestigungsmaterial.

title: VENDITORE– Appuntamenti Fissati – (Roma)
description:
**Stiamo strutturando la nostra rete vendita**

Cerchiamo persone che siano appassionate di vendita davvero: che vogliano chiudere trattative, fare numeri, portare risultati.  
Se non hai attitudine alla vendita o non sei disposto a metterti in gioco, non è il posto giusto.

Se sei un venditore che sa chiudere, o vuoi diventarlo, e vuoi lavorare su appuntamenti già fissati, senza perdere tempo con liste fredde e procacciagione, qui puoi fare la differenza.

Vuoi guadagnare davvero in base ai risultati e crescere in un contesto strutturato e meritocratico? Continua a leggere.

Design \& Sicurezza è un’azienda in forte crescita nel settore serramenti e outdoor di alta gamma, con un modello organizzato, supporto interno e flusso costante di appuntamenti qualificati.

Chi dimostra valore ha la possibilità concreta di crescere, strutturare un team e arrivare a gestire un’area commerciale.  
Non mettiamo limiti al percorso: li mettono solo i risultati.

**COSA FARAI**

* Incontrerai clienti già interessati, con appuntamenti fissati dall’azienda
* Effettuerai consulenza e vendita a domicilio o in showroom
* Gestirai la trattativa fino alla chiusura
* Lavorerai con un team tecnico e organizzato al tuo fianco

NON è porta a porta  
NON è richiesta attività principale di procacciamento a freddo

**PERCORSO DI INSERIMENTO**

In base al profilo:

* Apprendisti venditori
* Venditori esperti
* Inserimento diretto con sistema provvigionale
* Persone con forte attitudine commerciale

Percorso formativo fino a 6 mesi con rimborso di €1\.000/mese.  
Al termine inserimento nel ruolo commerciale con partita IVA.

**COSA OFFRIAMO**

* Formazione tecnica
* Appuntamenti giornalieri forniti dall’azienda
* Sistema provvigionale tra i più competitivi del settore
* Guadagni proporzionati ai risultati (indicativamente €3\.000 – €10\.000\+/mese)
* Supporto tecnico, commerciale e organizzativo
* Showroom e strumenti professionali
* Ambiente meritocratico con reale possibilità di crescita

**PERFORMANCE E GUADAGNI REALI**

Per trasparenza:

* Target minimo: €60\.000/mese di venduto (profilo senior)
* Provvigione maturata minima: €6\.000

I nostri commerciali più performanti:

* raggiungono €150\.000 – €200\.000/mese di venduto
* con provvigioni tra €15\.000 e €20\.000\+

Nei primi mesi è previsto un periodo di adattamento.  
Successivamente lavoriamo su obiettivi chiari, concreti e misurabili.

**REQUISITI FONDAMENTALI**

* Passione nella vendita diretta oppure forte attitudine commerciale
* Capacità di gestione della trattativa e orientamento alla chiusura
* Determinazione, ambizione e mentalità orientata ai risultati
* Disponibilità a lavorare su Roma e provincia
* Auto propria
* Disponibilità ad aprire partita IVA (se non già attiva)

**NON FA PER TE SE**

* Cerchi uno stipendio fisso senza responsabilità sui risultati
* Non sei disposto a metterti in gioco
* Non hai attitudine alla vendita
* Ti spaventa lavorare su obiettivi

**CHI STIAMO CERCANDO**

Cerchiamo persone che vogliono crescere, guadagnare e costruire qualcosa di serio.  
Non ci interessa riempire posizioni.  
Ci interessa inserire persone che sanno vendere o vogliono diventarlo davvero.

**CANDIDATURA**

Se pensi di essere in linea con quello che cerchiamo, candidati.  
Solo i profili ritenuti idonei verranno contattati per il colloquio, durante il quale verrà effettuato anche un test attitudinale.

Contratto di lavoro: Contratto con partita IVA, Tirocinio formativo/stage

Retribuzione: €1\.000,00 \- €10\.000,00 al mese

Esperienza:

* Vendite dirette: 1 anno (Preferenziale)

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Responsabile officina
description:
Responsabile Officina – Settore Automotive

Azienda strutturata operante nel settore ricambi auto, con officina interna specializzata in meccanica generale e servizio pneumatici, ricerca un Responsabile di Officina altamente qualificato.

Cerchiamo una figura operativa e decisionale, in grado di gestire in autonomia l’organizzazione dell’officina, coordinare il team e garantire standard elevati di efficienza e qualità.

Responsabilità:

Gestione completa delle attività di officina

Coordinamento e supervisione del personale operativo

Pianificazione degli interventi e ottimizzazione dei tempi di lavoro

Controllo qualità su lavorazioni e consegne

Gestione diretta del rapporto con i clienti

Risoluzione autonoma di problematiche tecniche complesse

Requisiti obbligatori:

Esperienza concreta e dimostrabile in officina meccanica e/o gommista

Ottima conoscenza del settore automotive (riparazioni, diagnosi, pneumatici)

Forte capacità organizzativa e orientamento ai risultati

Attitudine al problem solving e alla gestione dello stress

Capacità di utilizzo strumenti diagnostici avanzati

Costituisce requisito preferenziale:

Esperienza pregressa come responsabile officina

Orario di lavoro:

08:30 – 13:30

14:30 – 18:30

Offerta:

Inserimento in azienda solida e in crescita

Ruolo di responsabilità reale e autonomia decisionale

Retribuzione competitiva, commisurata all’esperienza

Nota: verranno presi in considerazione esclusivamente candidati con esperienza concreta nel settore.

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno

Retribuzione: €1\.400,00 \- €1\.600,00 al mese

Benefit:

* Distributori automatici di snack e bevande
* Orario flessibile
* Parcheggio libero
* Sconti sui prodotti o servizi dell'azienda

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Avvocato
description:
Siamo alla ricerca di un/una **Avvocato/a** qualificato/a e motivato/a da inserire nel nostro team legale interno.

**Chi siamo**  
Siamo una società dinamica e in crescita, operante in un contesto professionale strutturato e orientato all’innovazione. Offriamo un ambiente stimolante, con opportunità concrete di sviluppo professionale.

**Responsabilità principali**

* Fornire consulenza legale su questioni societarie, contrattuali e normative
* Redigere, revisionare e negoziare contratti
* Gestire contenziosi e rapporti con consulenti legali esterni
* Monitorare la conformità alle normative vigenti
* Supportare i diversi dipartimenti aziendali su tematiche legali

**Requisiti**

* Laurea in Giurisprudenza e abilitazione alla professione forense
* Esperienza pregressa
* Ottima conoscenza del diritto civile e commerciale
* Capacità di problem solving e orientamento al risultato
* Eccellenti doti comunicative e relazionali

**Costituiscono un plus**

* Esperienza in contesti aziendali o multinazionali
* Conoscenza della lingua inglese
* Specializzazione in diritto societario o compliance

**Cosa offriamo**

* Inserimento in un contesto professionale solido e strutturato
* Opportunità di crescita e formazione continua
* Retribuzione competitiva commisurata all’esperienza

Se sei interessato/a a entrare a far parte del nostro team, invia il tuo CV aggiornato e/o una breve lettera di presentazione.

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno

Benefit:

title: Process Improvement Lead
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

**DESCRIPTION:**  

The Process Improvement Lead is responsible for identifying, analyzing, and implementing continuous improvement initiatives within the Technical Services department (Maintenance, Engineering, Utilities, Calibration, and Equipment Qualification).


The role aims to enhance operational efficiency, GMP compliance, equipment reliability, and overall departmental performance. The position acts as a cross\-functional leader, ensuring alignment with site strategy, regulatory requirements, and corporate standards.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Identify improvement opportunities through KPI analysis
* Lead standardization and simplification initiatives across maintenance processes, technical change control, asset management, and capital project workflows.
* Support SOP harmonization and process optimization within a GMP\-regulated environment.
* Ensure that all improvement initiatives comply with GMP requirements and applicable regulations (EU GMP, FDA, etc.).
* Develop data\-driven tools for tracking performance and identifying trends.
* Support strategic planning activities within Technical Services by providing analytical insights and performance evaluations.
* Work closely with Production, Quality Assurance, Validation, EHS, and Project Management to ensure alignment of improvement initiatives.
* Contribute to digital transformation initiatives within the Technical Services function.


REQUIREMENTS:

**Education**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Industrial, Chemical, or equivalent).
* Lean / Six Sigma certification (Green Belt or Black Belt) preferred.
* Project Management certification (PMP or equivalent) considered a plus.

**Experience**

* Minimum 3 years of experience in a pharmaceutical industrial environment
* Proven experience in:


	+ Process improvement initiatives
	+ Maintenance and Engineering environments
	+ GMP\-compliant operations
* Experience managing cross\-functional projects.
* Strong knowledge of Lean, Six Sigma, and Root Cause Analysis methodologies.
* Good understanding of GMP regulations and technical change control processes.
* Excellent communication and facilitation abilities.
* Ability to operate effectively in complex and highly regulated environments.

**Languages**

* Fluent in Italian.
* Professional proficiency in English (written and spoken)
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: Supervisor, PDS Sterile Manufacturing
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Adherence to all Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) Safety Standards, Cleanroom: no hair products, jewelry, makeup, nail polish, perfume, exposed piercings, facial hair etc... allowed, Office, Some degree of PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) required (safety glasses, gowning, gloves, lab coat, ear plugs etc.)**Job Description**


At **Thermo Fisher Scientific**, you’ll do meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our team and help bring our Mission to life every day—enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. We empower our employees with the resources and opportunities to achieve their career goals while developing innovative solutions to some of the world’s toughest challenges, from protecting the environment to ensuring food safety and supporting the search for cancer treatments.

**Position Summary**
--------------------


We are seeking a **PDS Sterile Manufacturing Supervisor** to join our manufacturing operations team. In this role, you will lead and support production teams involved in sterile pharmaceutical manufacturing, ensuring operational excellence, product quality, and full compliance with cGMP standards and FDA regulations.This position combines strong technical expertise with people leadership. You will coordinate production activities, oversee manufacturing processes, and drive continuous improvement initiatives while fostering a culture of safety, quality, and accountability.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Supervise and coordinate daily sterile manufacturing operations
* Ensure compliance with cGMP, FDA, and company quality standards
* Monitor production performance and maintain adherence to schedules
* Review batch records and ensure accurate documentation
* Lead, coach, and develop manufacturing team members
* Support investigations, deviations, and corrective actions
* Promote a strong culture of safety, quality, and continuous improvement
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with Quality, Engineering, and other departments
* Identify and implement process and operational improvements

**Education**
-------------

* Bachelor’s degree in Science, Engineering, Chemistry, Economics, Business or a related STEM field (preferred)
* Equivalent combinations of education and experience (i.e. high school diploma and at least 7 years of sterile manufacturing experience)

**Experience**
--------------

* **3\+ years** of experience in pharmaceutical or biotechnology manufacturing,
* **2\+ years** of supervisory or leadership experience in a GMP\-regulated environment

**Preferred Qualifications**
----------------------------

* Strong knowledge of **cGMP regulations** and **FDA requirements**
* Experience with **aseptic processing** and **cleanroom operations**
* Familiarity with **environmental monitoring** and aseptic techniques
* Experience with **batch record review** and manufacturing documentation
* Knowledge of **Lean, Six Sigma**, or other continuous improvement methodologies
* Strong project management and organizational skills
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment
* Experience driving operational excellence and quality improvements
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office and manufacturing systems
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work cross\-functionally
